current_conditions.site_name: ETOWAH RIVER AT GA 9, NEAR DAWSONVILLE, GA

water_temp_read.read: Water is very warm for trout.

light_pressure_read.read: Light and pressure do not strongly push the outlook either way.

stocking_read.read: Recent stocking supports eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.

angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.

fly_reasoning.egg_pattern: Recent stocking makes eggs a logical confidence fly.
fly_reasoning.squirmy_worm: A simple stocked-trout attractor and good dirty-water option.
fly_reasoning.duracell: A strong attractor nymph when fish will move a little but are not fully aggressive.

caution_notes: Warm water may increase trout stress.

generated_text.quick_read: Trout fishing on the Etowah River is tough due to very warm water at 80.2°F and moderate flow. Recent stocking provides some opportunity using stocked-trout attractors and egg patterns.
generated_text.best_window: The day 2 morning window is best because cooler morning temperatures and moderate sky cover support more comfortable conditions for trout to feed near refuges and structure.
generated_text.current_conditions: Water temperature is very warm at 80.2°F, flow is 96.5 cfs, gage height is 3.68 ft, and turbidity is moderate at 6.4 NTU.
generated_text.weather_setup: Sky cover averages 20% with pressure steady at 30.05 inHg and a neutral trend. Winds are from the west at 4.8 mph with gusts up to 16.1 mph and no rain expected.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout will favor cold-water refuges, oxygenated spots, runs, seams, and structure while avoiding heat stress. Present smaller natural patterns and stocked-trout attractors with clean drifts. Use caution as warm water may increase trout stress.
